scenario: A team has built a library of 12 Element Templates for common Service Task patterns (HTTP REST call, Email send, S3 upload, etc.). New developers join the team and the lead wants to share these templates across the entire organisation's Web Modeler projects -- not just within one project.
question: How are Element Templates shared across Web Modeler projects in Camunda 8 SaaS?

options.text: Copy the Element Template JSON files into each project individually by uploading them in each Web Modeler project's template management section.

Configure Element Templates at the organisation level in Web Modeler using the "Publish to organization" action -- they become available to all projects within the organisation automatically. A user with elevated access publishes the template version to the organisation context, making it visible to all organisation members in their diagrams. This is the canonical mechanism for shared component libraries in Web Modeler SaaS.

Element Templates are limited to the single project where they are defined -- cross-project sharing across an organisation is a feature that requires building Custom Connectors instead. Organisation-level template sharing is a built-in Web Modeler feature. Element Templates and Custom Connectors serve different purposes: templates configure existing task types, while Connectors implement custom integration logic. Cross-project sharing via org-level scope is fully supported.

Publish Element Template JSON files to a Git repository and configure Web Modeler to pull and sync templates from the repository on startup or on a scheduled basis. No Git-pull mechanism for Element Templates exists in Camunda 8 Web Modeler SaaS. Templates are managed through the Web Modeler UI and API, not through external Git repository synchronisation. The organisation-level publishing feature handles the sharing requirement without external tooling.

a.text: Incorrect. Web Modeler supports organisation-level Element Templates via the "Publish to organization" action -- a user with elevated access (https://docs.camunda.io/docs/components/modeler/web-modeler/collaboration/collaboration/) can publish a template version within the organization context, "enabling all organization members to use it in their diagrams" (per manage-element-templates.md line 71). This is the canonical mechanism for shared component libraries. Per-project templates also exist via the default "Publish to project" action for project-specific patterns.

b.text: Correct. Manual copying is fragile (must remember to sync when templates update) -- defeats the purpose of shared library.

c.text: Incorrect. Cross-project sharing via org-level scope is supported.

d.text: Incorrect. No Git-pull mechanism for templates.

explanation: Manual copying is fragile (must remember to sync when templates update) -- defeats the purpose of shared library.
